response.justification: The role's single most critical requirement is 8 years of C++ programming experience, and the candidate has virtually none — C++ appears only in a ~6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project. The rest of the 15+ year career is built almost entirely on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, JavaScript, and web-stack tooling. Beyond the C++ disqualifier, the candidate lacks meaningful cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure architecture experience; their 'infrastructure' work is primarily application-layer DevOps (AWS CDK for pricing logic, Kubernetes for a small open-source GIS project). There is no evidence of technical leadership over infrastructure or compute teams at the required seniority.
strengths: Strong software-craftsmanship and TDD culture demonstrated consistently across multiple roles and public workshops
Genuine distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency DLang services
Breadth of mentoring and knowledge-sharing activity (Selenium courses, Refactoring Dojos, conference talks) shows real inclination toward technical leadership
concerns: C++ experience is essentially absent — estimated under one year total versus the 8-year minimum hard requirement
No cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure architecture ownership; AWS CDK usage was scoped to a pricing microservice, not platform engineering
No formal technical lead or staff-level infrastructure leadership role; 'Staff' titles appear in contexts focused on web/application development rather than platform or systems work
